prompt:
Ticket from the device-support rotation. The team pulls lines out of appliance journals all day, and the current grab bag of one-liners keeps burning people: someone pastes disk[0] or 10.2.30.4 into a -match and gets hits for disk0 and 10x2y30z4, or pastes it into -like and gets nothing because it wasn't a whole-line glob. I want one small tool, litmatch.ps1, in the workspace root, that makes the mode explicit every time:

    pwsh -NoProfile -File litmatch.ps1 -Path <file> -Pattern <text> -Mode <literal|wildcard|regex> [-CaseSensitive]

Semantics, exactly: literal mode treats the pattern as plain text and matches any line CONTAINING it -- brackets, dots, +, *, ?, $ in the pattern are just characters, full stop (use [regex]::Escape or [WildcardPattern]::Escape under the hood rather than hand-rolling a blacklist -- hand-rolled ones are how we got here). wildcard mode is a whole-line glob with -like semantics (*, ?, [chars] live). regex mode is an unanchored .NET regex search. Everything is case-insensitive unless -CaseSensitive is given. Matching lines print to stdout verbatim, file order, nothing else; no matches is still a clean exit 0. A pattern that isn't a valid regex in regex mode gets 'litmatch: invalid regex: <pattern>' on stderr and exit 65 instead of a stack trace; a mode outside the three is refused with a nonzero exit and no stdout; a missing file is 'litmatch: file not found: <path>' on stderr and exit 66.

The acceptance tests are test_litmatch.ps1 (pwsh -NoProfile -File test_litmatch.ps1) -- they encode the exact behavior table we agreed on in the ticket, including the disk[0] and 10.2.30.4 cases in all modes. Don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P10 item 4 (5E-style regex/wildcard escape boundary, BUILD kind: tests ship, implementation doesn't; behavior table probed on pwsh 7.6.3 2026-07-16 against the reference implementation -- all 16 cases verified by execution, not assumption). The pinned table walks the same pattern text across all three modes: disk[0] (literal hits the bracket lines, regex hits disk0 via char class), 10.2.30.4 (regex also hits 10x2y30z4), a+b (regex hits aab, literal hits a+b), cache*hot and *$ and what? vs what[?] on the wildcard side (whole-line -like semantics, [?] as the wildcard-native literalizer), plus ordinal case sensitivity both ways, invalid-regex 65 refusal, unknown-mode refusal, missing-file 66, and clean-empty-match exit 0. Reference implementation: literal = [regex]::Escape + IgnoreCase regex, wildcard = [System.Management.Automation.WildcardPattern] with WildcardOptions, regex = [regex]::new in try/catch; ValidateSet on -Mode. Differentiation vs wave-5 siblings ts-reglob and py-gaugelog (5E1 regex-escape seeds): the PowerShell-native pin is the three-way -like/-match/.Contains boundary in ONE language -- wildcard char classes ([0] matching disk0) exist in PS -like but not in JS regex or Python fnmatch siblings, and [WildcardPattern]::Escape/[regex]::Escape are the idiomatic literalizers the prompt demands. No wave-4 bash sibling does whole-line glob vs unanchored regex vs substring as a pinned contract. Verify run 5x during self-check, deterministic.
